courses.title: The Chicano and Latino in U.S. History from the 1960s to the Present

notes: COLLEGE ADMISSIONS REQUIREMENTS FOR TRANSFER STUDENTS THIS MAJOR IS OFFERED BY THE COLLEGE OF LETTERS AND SCIENCE (L&S) IMPORTANT information for students applying for admission to L&S for the fall 2025 application cycle and beyond Completion of Essential Skills requirements (Reading & Composition, Quantitative Reasoning, and Foreign Language) or Cal-GETC/IGETC will no longer be a requirement for admission; however, completion of either is strongly recommended prior to enrollment at UC Berkeley to ensure timely degree progress. More information on new polices is available on the L&S Transfer Student Applicants website. By the end of the spring term preceding fall enrollment at Berkeley, you must complete : 1) The 7-course pattern (as found in the UC Transfer Admission Eligibility Courses Menu in ASSIST): · Two transferable courses in English composition; · One transferable course in mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning; · Four transferable college courses chosen from at least two of the following subject areas: o arts and humanities o social and behavioral sciences o physical and biological sciences AND 2) Major Requirements : Follow the guidance on preparation for this major below. For more information on L&S General Education requirements: For information on courses that will satisfy L&S General Education (Essential Skills and Seven-Course Breadth) requirements from your community college complete the following steps: Go to "Search below for articulation agreements: Major, department, and campus-specific general education agreements” Click on the down arrow to select: Academic Year, Institution: UC Berkeley , and Agreements with Other Institutions: (Your CA Community College). Click on “View Agreements”. Under “View Agreement by” select “ General Education/Breadth ”. Click on the “General Education/Breadth” radio button, then click on “View Agreement” Learn more from the UC Berkeley Office of Undergraduate Admission and specifically about transfer admission preparation. Review all available majors at UC Berkeley .

The Chicano Studies major offers an interdisciplinary curriculum of academic study that critically examines the historical and contemporary experiences of people of Mexican descent in the context of American society and institutions; and studies particular aspects of Mexican history, culture and politics as they bear upon the Chicano community, past and present. Emphasis is given to the student developing a broad knowledge of the Chicano experience. thus, the major stresses the analysis of the interrelationships in the historical background, cultural patterns and artistic expression of the Chicano community in order to acquire a well-rounded, in-depth understanding of the contemporary interface between Chicanos and American society. In this connection, the major strives to incorporate various disciplines in its approach, such as political science, sociology, anthropology, history, literary criticism and art. Through the interdisciplinary nature of our curriculum, the major is aimed at preparing students for incorporation into the world of work and for a wide range of advanced graduate work and/or professional training in various fields. Please consult with the Chicano Studies adviser regarding the use of courses taken at your community college for credit toward the major. Generally, some UC-transferable courses taken at your community college can in fact be used to meet certain components of the major requirements. For more information contact Major Advisors: Dewey St. Germaine (510) 643-6420 deweystg@berkeley.edu Laura Jimenez-Olvera (510) 642-0243 lauraj@berkeley.edu https://ethnicstudies.berkeley.edu/
